name: Paul Sellers
bio: English woodworker and teacher known for hand-tool skills and his real woodworking video courses.

name: Peter Korn
bio: American furniture maker and founder of the Center for Furniture Craftsmanship in Maine.

name: Christian Becksvoort
bio: American furniture maker specializing in Shaker design and cherry casework; longtime contributor to Fine Woodworking.

name: James Krenov
bio: Russian-born American cabinetmaker and teacher who shaped the studio furniture movement.

name: Christopher Schwarz
bio: American woodworker editor and publisher at Lost Art Press; writes on hand tools and workbenches.

name: Tage Frid
bio: Danish-American master cabinetmaker and teacher; longtime professor at RISD.

name: Robert Wearing
bio: English craftsman and teacher who wrote clear guides on shop-made tools and hand-tool technique.

name: Garrett Hack
bio: American furniture maker and teacher in Vermont known for his work with hand planes.

name: Ernest Joyce
bio: British cabinetmaker whose reference on furniture making has been a standard for decades.

name: R. Bruce Hoadley
bio: Wood scientist and professor whose books explain wood structure and movement for woodworkers.

name: George Nakashima
bio: American woodworker and furniture maker who worked with the natural edge and grain of the tree.

name: Sam Maloof
bio: American furniture designer and woodworker famous for his sculpted rocking chairs.

name: Andy Rae
bio: American cabinetmaker and writer covering joinery and furniture construction methods.
top_work: The Complete Illustrated Guide to Furniture and Cabinet Construction

name: Gary Rogowski
bio: American woodworker and teacher who runs a school in Portland and writes on joinery.
top_work: The Complete Illustrated Guide to Joinery

name: Michael Pekovich
bio: American furniture maker and longtime art director at Fine Woodworking magazine.
top_work: The Why and How of Woodworking

name: Nancy Hiller
bio: British-American cabinetmaker and writer known for period kitchens and built-in casework.

name: Aldren A. Watson
bio: American illustrator and woodworker who documented hand tools and the traditional workshop.
top_work: Hand Tools: Their Ways and Workings

name: David Charlesworth
bio: English furniture maker and teacher known for precise hand-tool technique and plane setup.

name: Vic Tesolin
bio: Canadian woodworker and writer advocating a minimal, hand-tool approach to furniture.

name: Jim Tolpin
bio: American cabinetmaker and writer covering layout, geometry, and traditional workshop practice.
